pattern: Once you know which single value is smallest and which is largest, the rest of the array's total divided by its count is the whole answer -- no need to touch order.
hints: You only need two facts about the array: its minimum and its maximum value.
Sum every salary, then subtract the minimum and the maximum from that running total.
Divide the reduced sum by the reduced count (the original length minus two) to get the answer.

edge_inputs.description: minimum array length of three at the lowest allowed salary values
values near the upper salary bound
extremes are not at the ends of the given order
one very large outlier and one very small outlier among close values
middle values repeat-free but tightly clustered

stress.description: random unique salaries across the full allowed range and size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    vals = rng.sample(range(1000, 1000001), n)
    return [vals]
